app.title: Cognitive Arena
app.subtitle: Cross-temporal dialogue of ideas
home.hero.title: Choose an era and thinker on the world map
home.hero.subtitle: Pick a time period, then a region, to see active philosophers of that age.
home.map.hint: Click a region on the map
home.map.empty: No detailed philosopher data for this region in this period.
home.map.selectRegion: Select a region on the map
battle.tablePreparing: The debate table is being prepared…
battle.topicAiNote: Topic and views generated by the backend LLM
battle.stanceHint: You will enter a multi-round debate; keep entering arguments.
battle.roundHint: You debate directly with the philosopher; the judge observes and may step in to guide both sides.
battle.summaryFailed: Summary could not be generated. Check the backend and retry.
battle.choiceOpening: You chose "{choice}". Please state your reasoning for the first round.
battle.prompt.turnTask: The user just spoke. Direct debate: philosopherReplyToUser is required every round. As Judge you may intervene (judgeSpeaks) to guide both sides; addressTo marks who you mainly question (user or philosopher)—never silence the philosopher. If judgeSpeaks=true and addressTo=philosopher, philosopherReplyToJudge is required. Set continueDebate.
battle.prompt.summaryTask: Generate a full summary explanation from the debate history.
battle.prompt.turnCriteria: Return philosopherReplyToUser, judgeSpeaks, judgeMessage, addressTo, philosopherReplyToJudge, continueDebate
battle.prompt.philosopherToUserTask: The user just spoke. Reply as the philosopher in first person only. Do not output Judge content.
battle.prompt.judgeStepTask: Read the history including the latest user and philosopher messages. As Judge, decide whether to intervene. Do not speak as the philosopher.
battle.prompt.judgeStepCriteria: judgeSpeaks boolean; if false, judgeMessage=""; if true, non-empty judgeMessage and addressTo user|philosopher
battle.prompt.philosopherToJudgeTask: The Judge has questioned the philosopher (see last Judge line). Reply as the philosopher in first person to the Judge only.
battle.prompt.constraints: English; JSON only; continueDebate is boolean
error.topicJson: Model did not return valid debate topic JSON
error.turnJson: Model did not return valid debate turn JSON
disciplines.hero.title: Did you really think it through?
disciplines.hero.subtitle: Explore cognitive clashes in business, psychology, learning, and hot topics.
roundtable.pickerHint: Full catalog from Philosophy Debate — pick 2–4

roundtable.topic.ai-free-will.descriptio: AI, consciousness, and the nature of free choice
roundtable.topic.utopia.title: Should an ideal society pursue absolute equality or freedom?
roundtable.topic.truth.title: Does objective truth exist?
roundtable.topic.education.title: Should education train tools or whole persons?
roundtable.topic.education.description: Skills training vs. character formation today

disciplines.emptyCategory: No debates in this category yet. Try AI new topic.
discipline.battle.supportBuilderHint: The builder's view is more persuasive
discipline.battle.supportBreakerHint: The breaker's view is more persuasive
discipline.battle.uncertainHint: But I will explain my confusion
discipline.battle.explainReasonHint: This is not about picking an answer — explain why you chose as you did.
discipline.battle.reasonPlaceholder: Write your thinking: why do you support this view? What assumptions are you making? What factors did you weigh?
discipline.battle.reasonMinLength: Please write a more detailed reason (at least 10 characters).
discipline.battle.fullViewHint: After reflection, here is a more complete analysis
login.title: Login
login.subtitle: Enter your cognitive arena
profile.guestHint: Login to view your personalized mind analysis. Each user's profile is unique.
profile.title: Your Mind Profile
profile.subtitle: Understand your way of thinking
profile.biases.title: Your Cognitive Bias Map
profile.biases.description: Based on your choices and reasoning in debates. Recognizing your biases is the first step to improvement.
profile.biases.emptyHint: Participate in a few debates to see your cognitive bias analysis.
profile.insights.title: Key Insights
profile.insights.warningTemplate: {bias} is your main cognitive bias ({percentage}%). {description}. Try actively seeking evidence that challenges your views.
profile.insights.warningFallback: Keep participating in debates. The system will analyze your cognitive bias patterns.
profile.insights.strengthTemplate: You're willing to change your stance ({count} times), showing an open mind. This is an important quality for rational thinking.
profile.insights.strengthFallback: Stay open-minded. Willingness to adjust your stance based on new evidence is key to rational thinking.
profile.recent.title: Recent Thinking Records
profile.recent.empty: No battle records yet. Go join a debate!
profile.cta.quote: "How immature I used to think" — that's the source of growth
dilemma.title: Moral Dilemmas
dilemma.subtitle: Discuss moral dilemmas with philosophers
dilemma.selectPhilosopher: Choose a Philosopher to Discuss With
dilemma.selectPhilosopherHint: After clicking a philosopher card, you'll enter a discussion about the current dilemma.
dilemma.discussionHint: Discussions follow a "Philosopher Response + Judge Follow-up" format, consistent with the debate window.
dilemma.inputPlaceholder: Continue questioning {name}, or add your reasoning...
dilemma.summary.title: Full Analysis
dilemma.summary.failed: Model failed to generate summary. Please check backend and Bailian configuration.
dilemma.error.philosopherResponse: Model did not return valid philosopher response JSON
roundtable.error.openingJson: Model did not return valid opening JSON
roundtable.error.contentFilter: Generation was blocked by content safety. Try a different topic or philosophers.
roundtable.error.replyJson: Model did not return valid roundtable reply JSON
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.title: Trolley Problem
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.subtitle: When utility clashes with responsibility, would you pull the lever?
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.imageCaptio: A runaway trolley heads toward five people. You can pull a lever to divert it onto another track where one person stands.
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.question: If you were standing by the switch right now, what would you do?
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.promptLead: Discuss sacrificing the few to save the many, action, responsibility, and moral limits.
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.option.pull: Pull the lever — sacrifice one to save five
Maximize outcomes and accept responsibility for intervening.
I would pull the lever to save five lives at the cost of one.
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.option.do-n: Do not pull the lever — do not intervene
Actively harming someone is not morally equivalent to failing to act.
I would not pull the lever because actively causing one death is unacceptable.
dilemma.case.trolley-problem.option.seek: Reject the forced binary and look for systemic or situational alternatives.
I would try to find a third way rather than accept the dilemma as given.
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.title: Brain in a Vat
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.subtitle: If all experience could be simulated, can you still know you live in reality?
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.imageAlt: Illustration of brain in a vat
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.imageCaption: A brain kept alive by life support, fed neural signals indistinguishable from ordinary experience.
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.question: Facing this scenario, which attitude would you take?
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.promptLead: Discuss sources of knowledge, sensory experience, skepticism, and judgments about reality.
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.option.trust: Trust experience as a provisional basis for reality
Even if experience might be simulated, we need stable experience for thought and action.
I would still treat stable experience as my basis for reality because cognition and action require it.
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.option.radic: Demand stronger proof — otherwise stay skeptical
Sensory experience is unreliable; reality cannot be guaranteed by experience alone.
Without stronger proof, I would remain fundamentally skeptical about the world's reality.
dilemma.case.brain-in-a-vat.option.pragm: Truth uncertain — live in the actionable world anyway
Prioritize agency and intelligibility over ultimate metaphysical certainty.
I accept uncertainty but will live in this actionable, communicative world.
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.title: Veil of Ignorance
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.subtitle: If you did not know who you would be, how would you design society?
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.imageAlt: Illustration of the veil of ignorance
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.imageCapt: Before choosing rules, you do not know your place in society.
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.question: What kind of institutional design would you favor?
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.promptLea: Discuss fairness, liberty, institutional risk, and protection of the worst off.
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.option.pr: Place the security and dignity of the least advantaged at the center of design.
I would prioritize the worst off because behind the veil I must prepare for the worst position.
Institutions should secure basic freedoms first, then fair opportunity.
I would prioritize basic liberty and opportunity to prevent power from crushing individuals.
dilemma.case.veil-of-ignorance.option.ba: Balance liberty and fairness — allow limited inequality
Accept difference only if it improves outcomes for all, including the worst off.
I favor balancing liberty and fairness with limited inequality that benefits everyone.
dilemma.judge.opening: You chose "{choice}". Explain your reasoning and respond to the sharpest objection to your view.
